question: Which of the following statements is not a requirement for the SSI Divemaster rating?

options.a: Have logged at least 60 open water dives
options.c: Pass the Science of Diving final exam
options.d: Have the Diver Stress & Rescue certification

explanation: The minimum age for SSI Divemaster is 18, not 21. The other three are real requirements: Diver Stress & Rescue prerequisite, Science of Diving final exam, and 60+ logged open water dives.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 9 Dive Professional Standards

question: Assistant Instructors may teach, supervise and issue certifications/recognition cards for the following programs:

options.a: Scuba Explorers, Try Scuba, Scuba Skills Update, and Deep Diving
options.b: Scuba Explorers, Try Scuba, and all SSI Specialty programs
options.c: Scuba Explorers, Basic Diver, Scuba Skills Update, and Open Water Diver
options.d: Scuba Explorers, Try Scuba, Scuba Skills Update, and Perfect Buoyancy

explanation: AIs have direct teaching/certifying authority for the lowest-load programs: Scuba Explorers, Try Scuba, the Scuba Skills Update refresher, and Perfect Buoyancy. Deep Diving and OW Diver remain Open Water Instructor and above; not all specialties are AI-accessible.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 9 Dive Professional Standards

question: Which of the following is not a prerequisite for the SSI Master Instructor Rating?

options.b: Have logged at least 250 open water dives
options.c: Train primarily SSI programs at all dive professional levels
options.d: Be an active status Assistant Instructor Trainer

explanation: 150 certifications issued is NOT a Master Instructor prerequisite — the actual SSI MI cert count threshold is different (higher). The real requirements include 250+ logged OW dives, training primarily SSI programs at all professional levels, and being an active-status Assistant Instructor Trainer.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 9 Dive Professional Standards

question: How can a SSI Divemaster earn an Ecology Specialty Instructor certification?

options.a: Complete a Specialty Seminar or the Verification Application
options.c: Complete a Specialty Seminar, or co-teach the program
options.d: Complete the Verification application, or co-teach the program

explanation: An SSI Divemaster can earn the Ecology Specialty Instructor rating by either completing a Specialty Seminar or co-teaching the program with an active SSI specialty instructor. Verification Application is for cross-agency credential conversion (not the DM-to-specialty-instructor upgrade path); 'only co-teaching' is too narrow.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 9 Dive Professional Standards

question: An Assistant Instructor Trainer candidate must have issued at least:

options.a: 10 Scuba Diver and/or Open Water certifications
options.b: 20 Scuba Diver and/or Open Water certifications
options.c: 40 Scuba Diver and/or Open Water certifications
options.d: 30 Scuba Diver and/or Open Water certifications

explanation: An SSI Assistant Instructor Trainer candidate must have issued at least 30 Scuba Diver and/or Open Water certifications — meaningful classroom-and-water teaching experience, but lower than the higher MI thresholds.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 9 Dive Professional Standards

question: Assistant Instructors may qualify to teach, supervise and issue certifications for the following programs:

options.a: Boat Diving, Navigation, and Wreck Diving
options.b: Equipment Techniques, Wreck Diving, and Enriched Air Nitrox
options.c: Equipment Techniques, Navigation, and Wreck Diving
options.d: Equipment Techniques, Boat Diving, and Navigation

explanation: AIs may qualify to teach the three low-overhead-environment, low-risk specialties: Equipment Techniques, Boat Diving, and Navigation. Wreck Diving (overhead exposure) and Enriched Air Nitrox (gas management critical) are restricted to OWI and above.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 9 Dive Professional Standards

question: Prior to participating in the Instructor Evaluation, an Open Water Instructor candidate must:

options.b: Log at least 125 open water dives
options.c: Assist with at least 5 Open Water Diver programs
options.d: Complete at least 10 Try Scuba programs

explanation: Completion of the Instructor Training Course (ITC) is the immediate prerequisite for entering the Instructor Evaluation (IE). The ITC delivers the academic + presentation + skill-circuit training; the IE is the independent evaluation event. The other options aren't standalone IE prerequisites.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 9 Dive Professional Standards

question: After successful completion of the Instructor Evaluation, an Open Water Instructor is qualified to teach, supervise and issue certifications for:
explanation: After successful IE completion, an SSI Open Water Instructor is automatically qualified to teach all three: AOWD (built from OW-level specialties), Enriched Air Nitrox, and the Divemaster pro program. The full teaching authority is granted at OWI level — higher pro ratings (AIT, MI) extend WHO can be trained as instructors, not what programs can be taught.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 9 Dive Professional Standards

question: The SSI Divemaster program may be taught by:

options.a: A retired status Open Water Instructor
options.b: An inactive status Decompression Diving Instructor
options.d: An active status Dive Guide Instructor

explanation: DM is a Pro-level program — only an active-status Pro-level instructor (Dive Guide Instructor or higher / equivalent) can teach it. Retired and inactive statuses cannot teach. Assistant Instructors do not have authority to teach the DM program.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 9 Dive Professional Standards

question: To return to active status after being in canceled status for 3–5 years, the SSI Professional must:

options.a: Review the Open Water Diver Instructor Manual
options.b: Co-teach at least one Open Water Diver program
options.c: Pay three years of renewal fees
options.d: Pay the SSI Reactivation Fee and pass the final exam for the highest professional certification

explanation: After 3–5 years in canceled status the Pro must pay the SSI Reactivation Fee AND re-qualify by passing the final exam for their highest professional certification. The exam re-establishes current knowledge of standards and procedures, which evolve substantially over a multi-year gap.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 9 Dive Professional Standards

question: Snorkel Instructor program candidates must be:
explanation: Snorkel Instructor candidates must be at least 18 years old. The minimum age is the explicit gate — neither full SSI Pro status nor OWI rating is required to take the program. (Active Pro status would be a prerequisite for some upgrade paths but isn't the answer to the age-versus-status framing.)
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 9 Dive Professional Standards

question: What are the requirements for a Divemaster Instructor?

options.a: Hold an active status SSI certification for Night and Limited Visibility Instructor
options.c: Hold an active status SSI certification for Deep Diving Instructor
options.d: Have issued least 25 certifications from any recreational program

explanation: All three are required for the Divemaster Instructor rating: an active Deep Diving Instructor cert, an active Night and Limited Visibility Instructor cert, and at least 25 issued certifications from any recreational program. The combined teaching/specialty experience is the floor for moving above standalone OWI into pro-development territory.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 9 Dive Professional Standards

question: What is the minimum required rating to be a React Right Instructor?

options.c: No SSI Professional status is required

explanation: React Right is SSI's first-aid/CPR/AED program — separate from the diving curriculum. To become a React Right Instructor no SSI Professional (diving) status is required at all; the rating is open to anyone who completes the React Right Instructor pathway, because no diving authority is involved.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 9 Dive Professional Standards

question: Which of the following is not required to complete the Assistant Instructor Training Course?

options.a: Have logged at least 75 open water dives totaling 50 hours or more
options.b: Complete the Candidate Water Fitness Evaluation
options.c: Complete and pass all requirements from the Open Water Diver Instructor Manual
options.d: Complete a final exam with a score of 90% or higher

explanation: Completing the OW Diver Instructor Manual requirements is an Open Water Instructor (ITC/IE) task, not an AITC task. AITC candidates must log 75+ OW dives (≥50 hours), pass the Candidate Water Fitness Evaluation, and achieve 90%+ on the AITC final exam — but they do not work through the full OWI manual at this stage.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 9 Dive Professional Standards
